name: Ricky Nelson
title: Last known photo of Ricky Nelson, taken on the tarmac at Guntersville Municipal Airport in Alabama on December 31, 1985, about two hours before the plane crash that killed him.
description: Ricky Nelson and his fiancée Helen Blair were photographed together on the tarmac at Guntersville Municipal Airport in Alabama on the afternoon of December 31, 1985, roughly two hours before they boarded his vintage Douglas DC-3 for the flight to Dallas, Texas. En route, an in-flight fire broke out in the cabin and the aircraft crash-landed in a field near De Kalb, Texas. Nelson, Blair, and five members of his band were killed when smoke and flames overwhelmed the passenger cabin; both pilots survived by escaping through a cockpit window. The NTSB officially attributed the fire to a malfunctioning cabin heater. He was 45 years old.

lyrics.text:
I'm a travelin' man, I've made a lot of stops
All over the world
And in every part I own the heart
Of at least one lovely girl

Pretty Polynesian baby over the sea
I remember the night
When we walked in the sands of the Waikiki
And I held you oh so tight

Oh, I'm a travelin' man
Yes, I'm a travelin' man

bio: Eric Hilliard “Ricky” Nelson was an American rock and roll musician, singer-songwriter, and actor. As a child he starred alongside his family on the long-running television sitcom The Adventures of Ozzie and Harriet (1952–1966); as a teenager he became one of the first true rock and roll idols, placing 19 Top 10 singles on the Billboard charts between 1957 and 1962, including “Poor Little Fool,” “Travelin' Man,” and “Hello Mary Lou.” He was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1987.
additionalImagesNote: Plate 02: Nelson with musician Pat Upton at the same airport, also from December 31, 1985. Upton had been invited on the New Year's Eve flight to Dallas but declined due to business obligations, a decision that saved his life.

location.name: Guntersville Municipal Airport, 405 Buck Island Rd, Guntersville, AL 35976
